title: requests — field-level data classification (DTO wire fields)
description: This module owns NO tables and therefore has no direct row-level data to classify. The tables it PROJECTS FROM are owned by workflow/approvals + access/grants, which own their own data-classes declarations. This file classifies the FIELDS surfaced on the wire via the AccessRequestData DTO so the compliance module's DSAR + right-to-erasure orchestrators know what to include when a subject requests their access-request activity.

tiers.public: Non-identifying, safe to expose in logs / URLs.
tiers.internal: Platform-internal state. Never surfaced to end users. Not personally identifiable.
tiers.confidential: PII exportable on DSAR and erasable on request. Encrypted in transit + AES-256 at rest (in the OWNING module's tables).
tiers.restricted: Credential-adjacent secrets. Not applicable to this module.

confidential: AccessRequestData.reason — the human-authored rationale often carries PII
AccessRequestData.decisions[].comment — approver rationale often mentions colleagues
AccessRequestData.resource.display_name — e.g. an athlete's name is PII, an invoice's reference is not — classified conservatively at the confidential tier

dsar.exportOrchestration: The compliance module's DSAR orchestrator queries the underlying approval_instances + approval_decisions + access_grants rows filtered on the subject's user_id (as either requester or approver). This module contributes the `AccessRequestPresenter` binding that converts the raw rows into the DTO shape included in the export archive.

erasureBehaviour.confidential: Erasure orchestrated by the OWNING modules (workflow/approvals + access/grants). See retention.json. This module surfaces the erased fields as anonymised strings in DTO output (`display_name='[erased]'`, `email='erased-<hash>@invalid.local'`).
erasureBehaviour.internal: Internal fields naming an actor are anonymised together with the confidential ones — the reference stays (for graph integrity) but the identity is redacted.

dtoContractShapes.description: The additional DTOs used at the write path. Every field's tier is inferred from the classification above.

rows.description: Per-group aggregate metrics: submissions_count, approved_count, rejected_count, expired_count, cancelled_count, approval_rate, average_time_to_decision_hours, median_time_to_decision_hours. Aggregates only — individual user identity is not exposed at this tier.
